name: Hyliotidae
description: Hyliotas are a small family of African passerine birds that resemble warblers or flycatchers. They are active insectivores found in woodlands and savannas across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Hyliota
children.description: A genus of small, active birds with blue-black or violet upperparts and pale underparts, found in African woodlands.

children.name: Hyliota flavigaster
children.description: The Yellow-bellied Hyliota has glossy blue-black upperparts and bright yellow underparts. It inhabits miombo and other woodlands across central and southern Africa.

children.name: Hyliota australis
children.description: The Southern Hyliota has glossy blue-black upperparts and white underparts with a buffy wash. It is found in woodlands of eastern and southern Africa.

children.name: Hyliota usambarae
children.description: The Usambara Hyliota is a small songbird with glossy blue-black upperparts and whitish underparts. It is endemic to the Usambara Mountains of Tanzania and is considered endangered due to habitat loss.

children.name: Hyliota violacea
children.description: The Violet-backed Hyliota has glossy violet-blue upperparts and pale grey underparts. It inhabits lowland forests of west and central Africa.
